cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question. The task is to determine which of the four statements (A, B, C, D) is true based on the provided passage about the collapse of the Sacramento River salmon run. First, I'll go through each option one by one and cross-reference them with the passage. Starting with option (A): "Last fall, there was the second lowest number of grown salmon returning on record." The passage states, "only about 90,000 adult salmon returned to the Central Valley last fall--the second lowest number on record." So this seems correct. The term "grown salmon" here probably refers to adult salmon, which is what the passage mentions. So (A) might be true. Option (B): "The salmon migrates to the ocean when they grow up." The passage says, "wild salmon are born in streams and rivers, migrate to the ocean when they're juveniles..." The key here is "juveniles" versus "grow up." Juveniles are young fish, not fully grown. So the statement in (B) is incorrect because the migration happens when they're still young, not when they're fully grown. Therefore, (B) is false. Option (C): "The farm-raised salmon is not as delicious and nutrients as the wild salmon." The passage mentions that farm-raised salmon will be more available, but it doesn't compare the taste or nutritional value with wild salmon. The statement about being "not as delicious" or "nutrients" is not addressed in the text, so this is an assumption not supported by the passage. Thus, (C) is not a correct answer based on the given information. Option (D): "Salmon fishing off the coasts of California and Oregon is strictly forbidden."
